steps.title: Find direct diet evidence
steps.content: The researchers claim that the Pantanal jaguars can survive on more fish and aquatic reptiles than mammals. The strongest support would show what the jaguars actually ate, not merely what prey was available.

steps.title: Reject indirect support
steps.content: B shows only that aquatic reptiles were abundant, C gives a nutritional fact, and D still focuses on mammal prey. The remaining finding uses waste-matter evidence to show aquatic prey remains appeared more often than mammal remains. The answer is A.
